subject: [PATCH v2 04/14] mm: add VM_UFFD_RWP VMA flag

reviews.author: Mike Rapoport
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch adds a new VM_UFFD_RWP VMA flag but does not include any checks for it, and suggested this is worth considering.

Reviewer requested that mode bits and protection bits be grouped together in the VMA flag definitions, suggesting a reorganization of the enum values to maintain consistency
reviews.raw_body:
And nothing check for it as well, am I right?
Worth noting here, IMHO.

---

Nit: can we keep mode bits together and protection bits together here and
in the below changes?

Otherwise looks good to me

Reviewed-by: Mike Rapoport (Microsoft) <rppt@kernel.org>

2026-05-12.patch_summary: This patch adds a new VMA flag, VM_UFFD_RWP, to enable userfaultfd read-write protection (RWP). RWP extends the existing write-protection mechanism to also trap read accesses through userfaultfd. The flag is only meaningful when both PAGE_NONE and uffd PTE bit primitives exist. A new Kconfig symbol, CONFIG_USERFAULTFD_RWP, auto-selects when certain conditions are met. The patch includes changes to various files, including mm.h, proc.rst, task_mmu.c, userfaultfd_k.h, trace/events/mmflags.h, and Kconfig.

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch adds a new VMA flag, VM_UFFD_RWP, to enable userfaultfd read-write protection (RWP). RWP extends the existing write-protection mechanism to also trap read accesses through userfaultfd. The flag is only meaningful when both PAGE_NONE and uffd PTE bit primitives exist. A new Kconfig symbol, CONFIG_USERFAULTFD_RWP, auto-selects when certain conditions are met. The patch includes changes to various files, including mm.h, proc.rst, task_mmu.c, userfaultfd_k.h, trace/events/mmflags.h, and Kconfig.
